event.summary: Entered validation with a deterministic command, instruction, graph, and device-impact plan.

blockers.resume_when: Correct the failure and explicitly resume the unit.
Correct the failure and explicitly resume the unit.

acceptance.proof: The baseline inventory and deterministic comparison retain every effective gate, criterion, claim, and does-not-prove limitation except an explicitly identified semantic delta; none is currently authorized.
Current gates and quick/standard/deep/ci profiles derive only from the schema-valid policy; CI, wrappers, prose, receipts, and historical indexes cannot create a competing current inventory.
Orphan, duplicate, stale, malformed, bypassed, overlap, documentation drift, archive mutation, disappearing claims/limitations, and profile divergence reject deterministically.
Complete Rust tests, public boundary, activation/dependency closure, current owner gates, pinned historical replay, and exact documentation reconstruction pass through one policy facade.
Exact scope, lifecycle, instruction surfaces, public/private boundary, inert activation, and append-only evidence remain valid.

document.commit_policy: Commit proposal and lifecycle separately; then inventory/schema/policy/dispatcher, archive/docs/ADR/instruction routing, damaged coverage, validation, acceptance, and no-force publication evidence while preserving the pre-claim failure privately and append-only status publicly only through portable workflow evidence.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route validation impact classification and the sole current policy without retaining a second command inventory.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route contributors to the compact validation facade without expanding capability claims.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run documentation router and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Archive the prior guide exactly and replace it with the deterministic current policy projection.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run exact archive reconstruction and documentation drift tests.

instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed installed skill and work-environment 0.4.0 routing.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Existing guidance already requires explicit authority, ADR confirmation, damaged validation, receipts as evidence, and semantic claim bounds.

non_scope: Rust production behavior, public API, protocol bytes, discovery, clock, handshake, sample transport, buffering, recovery, runtime breadth, dependencies, features, devices, adapters, external implementation intake, or Manifold authority.
Rewriting accepted receipts, historical checkers, historical manifests, LSLC-003J/K/L/M evidence, failed attempts, or chronological workflow history.

Removing or weakening any current gate, criterion, claim, limitation, public boundary, project-workflow check, activation closure, or pinned replay without a separately explicit machine-readable semantic-delta decision.
Private recovery paths, account identity, machine-local paths, or recovery capsule bytes in public artifacts.
document.objective: Inventory and migrate the complete current validation semantics into one machine-readable policy authority and one dispatching facade while preserving pinned historical evidence, exact documentation history, all currently enforced behavior, claims, and limitations.
outputs: A bounded semantic inventory reconciles commands, criteria, claims, limitations, profiles, CI, manifests, live LSLC-003J/K/L/M consumers, units, focused gates, and accepted receipts.

One schema-validated current policy owns stable gates, profiles, dependencies, scope, claim bounds, environments, timeouts, state, and explicit semantic deltas; a separate immutable index binds pinned history.
One dispatcher drives local and CI profiles; compact deterministic documentation routes to an exact byte-preserved archive; an ADR records the durable authority change.

Damaged and drift tests reject missing claims or limitations, orphans, duplicates, stale/malformed/bypassed policy, historical/current overlap, competing inventories, documentation drift, and archive damage.
